title: Baggage Allowance
heroImageAlt: Travel luggage at an airport check-in
intro: Baggage rules vary by airline, route and fare brand. This guide explains the typical allowances you will see and how to add more if you need it.

sections.p:
Most international economy fares allow ONE cabin bag of 7 kg with maximum dimensions around 55 × 35 × 25 cm, plus a small personal item (handbag, laptop bag) that fits under the seat in front of you.

Premium economy typically allows 10 kg, business class 14 kg, and first class up to 18 kg. Some carriers (especially low-cost) measure your cabin bag at the gate — if it is over the weight or size, you may be asked to check it in for a fee.

Checked allowances on international flights from Sri Lanka typically range from 20 kg on lighter low-cost fares to 30 kg on full-service economy and 40 kg or more in business class. Long-haul routes to North America often use the PIECE concept (2 × 23 kg pieces) instead of the weight concept.

The fare brand label on your booking shows the exact allowance the airline confirmed for your ticket. If you are flying multiple airlines on one itinerary, the most restrictive allowance often applies — check before you pack.

Pre-purchasing extra baggage online is almost always 30–60% cheaper than paying at the airport. Add bags during booking, from Manage Booking, or by contacting us up to several hours before departure.

If you arrive at the airport with bags over the allowance, the airline charges per kilogram at the airport rate. The rate varies but is typically USD 15–60 per kg on international routes.

BICYCLES, GOLF CLUBS, SURFBOARDS, SKIS and MUSICAL INSTRUMENTS travel as special baggage and usually need to be declared in advance. Many airlines have specific size and weight limits and charge a flat fee. Tell our team and we will pre-clear it with the operating airline.

MOBILITY AIDS (wheelchairs, walking aids) travel free of charge on international flights. Inform us at booking so we can request airport assistance.

INFANTS: most airlines allow ONE cabin bag and one stroller free of charge for an infant on lap.

POWER BANKS, SPARE LITHIUM BATTERIES and E-CIGARETTES must travel in CABIN baggage, never in checked bags. Many airlines limit power-bank capacity to 100 Wh (around 27000 mAh) without prior approval.

Prohibited items include explosives, flammable liquids and gases, toxic substances, and sharp objects in cabin baggage. Liquids in cabin baggage must be in containers of 100 ml or less and fit in a single transparent 1-litre bag.

Duty-free liquids over 100 ml are allowed in cabin baggage if sealed in a security tamper-evident bag with the receipt — but be aware that some transit airports remove these at security.

sections.h: What to do if your bag is lost or delayed
sections.p:
Report it BEFORE leaving the baggage hall — go to the airline’s baggage office and complete a Property Irregularity Report (PIR). Keep your luggage tag and the PIR reference. Most bags are reunited within 24 to 48 hours.

Keep receipts for essential items you buy in the meantime (toiletries, basic clothing) — most airlines reimburse a reasonable amount under their conditions of carriage.
